vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1415: Stuck-agent auto-poke and auto-restart on extended inactivity

plan.title: Stuck-agent auto-poke and auto-restart on extended inactivity
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: The deacon's health classifier already labels work agents 'stuck' after 30 minutes of no activity, but nothing acts on that signal. With 8+ work agents in flight, a stuck agent silently blocks its PR's downstream lifecycle until a human notices and runs `pan kill` or `pan tell` by hand. The remediation hooks (`messageAgent`, `resumeAgent`, `markAgentTroubled`) all already exist — they just need a patrol that escalates through them on a deterministic schedule.
narratives.Proposal:
Add a new patrol `checkStuckAgentRemediation` (in a new module `src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation.ts`, called from `runPatrol()` in `deacon.ts`). For each running work agent that is idle (Stop-hook authoritative) and has no other patrol that should act first (bead-nudge, dead-end, first-completion):

1. At ≥20 min idle and `lastStage < 1` — call `messageAgent(agentId, pokeText)` and persist `lastStage=1`.
2. At ≥45 min idle and `lastStage < 2` (no activity since stage 1) — call `resumeAgent(agentId, recoveryMessage)`; only advance `lastStage=2` if the resume succeeds.
3. At ≥90 min idle and `lastStage < 3` (still no activity since stage 2) — call `markAgentTroubled(agentId)` and stop poking. The dashboard surfaces troubled agents for human triage.

State is per-agent at `~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/stuck-remediation.json` (siblings: health.json, .last-bead-nudge). It auto-resets when `runtime.lastActivity > firstStuckAt`. Config under `cloister.toml`'s new `stuck_remediation` block: `enabled`, `stage1_minutes` (default 20), `stage2_minutes` (45), `stage3_minutes` (90). Every transition logs `[deacon] stuck-remediation stage=N issue=... idleMin=...` via `logDeaconEventSync` and the addLog patrol-log stream.

autoDecisions.summary: Config lives in cloister.toml (not config.yaml as the issue body shows).
autoDecisions.rationale: All other deacon/patrol thresholds (`thresholds`, `auto_actions`, `monitoring`, `auto_restart`) live in `~/.panopticon/cloister.toml`. config.yaml is the per-project/global runtime+roles config — wrong layer for a patrol threshold. Following the existing convention makes the new config discoverable next to its siblings and consistent with how `pan-tldr` and similar deacon features are configured.

autoDecisions.summary: Skip stage 1 when `bd ready -l <issue>` returns open beads.
autoDecisions.rationale: `nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads` (10-min cooldown, more targeted message) already handles open-bead idle. Stuck-remediation should not double-poke. The new patrol covers the residual case: no open beads but agent still idle (e.g. agent forgot to call `pan done`, or it's mid-investigation without bead commits).
`checkDeadEndAgents` already nudges those specific dead-end shapes with feedback-aware messaging. Stuck-remediation is a generic safety net — it should defer to specialized patrols when they apply.

autoDecisions.summary: Stage 3 calls `markAgentTroubled` only; it does NOT kill the tmux session.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue says 'don't keep poking' and 'mark agent troubled,' not 'kill it.' The existing `markAgentTroubled` API (in `src/lib/agents.ts:845`) sets `state.troubled = true`, which the dashboard surfaces and which gates `start`/`resume`/`messageAgent`. The session staying alive lets the operator attach to inspect what the agent was last doing — destructive cleanup belongs to `pan kill`/`pan wipe`, not to a patrol.

Sibling to existing `health.json` and `.last-bead-nudge` cooldown files. Survives deacon restarts (so stage progression doesn't reset and re-poke the same idle agent every patrol cycle). Auto-removed by the existing `cleanupStaleAgentState` sweep when the agent dir is purged.

The issue's primary use case is the user's running pipeline; turning the patrol off by default would defeat the purpose. Operators can flip it off in cloister.toml. Default values (20/45/90 min) match the issue body.

autoDecisions.summary: Patrol function accepts an optional `now?: number` arg for test injection.
autoDecisions.rationale: Project rule (CLAUDE.md) mandates `vi.useFakeTimers()` for time-based code. Accepting `now` makes stage-transition tests deterministic without needing to mock Date.now globally, which simplifies the test bead and matches the pattern other deacon helpers already use.

autoDecisions.summary: Stage 3 escalation is also gated by the issue's tracker status: skip if mergeStatus=merged or readyForMerge=true.
autoDecisions.rationale: An agent that's already finished and is waiting for merge isn't 'stuck' in a remediable sense. The merge/close-out flow owns its lifecycle from there. Skipping prevents 'troubled' from being mis-applied to a working pipeline.

items.title: Add stuck_remediation config block to CloisterConfig + cloister.toml defaults

narrative.Action: Extend `CloisterConfig` in `src/lib/cloister/config.ts` with a new optional block `stuck_remediation?: StuckRemediationConfig` containing `{ enabled: boolean; stage1_minutes: number; stage2_minutes: number; stage3_minutes: number }`. Add the matching `StuckRemediationConfig` interface. Set defaults `{ enabled: true, stage1_minutes: 20, stage2_minutes: 45, stage3_minutes: 90 }` in `DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG`. Verify the deep-merge path handles a missing block (loads defaults) and a partial block (deep-merges).

subItems.title: `StuckRemediationConfig` interface exported from `src/lib/cloister/config.ts` with the four fields named exactly `enabled`, `stage1_minutes`, `stage2_minutes`, `stage3_minutes` (snake_case to match the rest of cloister.toml)
`CloisterConfig.stuck_remediation` field added (optional)
`DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG.stuck_remediation` set to `{ enabled: true, stage1_minutes: 20, stage2_minutes: 45, stage3_minutes: 90 }`
`loadCloisterConfigSync()` returns the defaults when no `[stuck_remediation]` block exists in cloister.toml
`loadCloisterConfigSync()` deep-merges a partial `[stuck_remediation]` block (e.g. setting only `stage1_minutes = 10`) with the defaults for the other fields
TypeScript build passes (`npm run typecheck`) and existing config tests in `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/config.test.ts` still pass

items.title: Add per-agent stuck-remediation state file helpers

narrative.Action: Create `src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation-state.ts` with three exported helpers operating on `~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/stuck-remediation.json`: `readStuckRemediationState(agentId): StuckRemediationState | null`, `writeStuckRemediationState(agentId, state): void`, `clearStuckRemediationState(agentId): void`. The state shape is `{ lastStage: 0 | 1 | 2 | 3; lastStageAt: string /* ISO */; firstStuckAt: string /* ISO */ }`. Use `existsSync` + `readFileSync`/`writeFileSync` (CLI-process-safe — these helpers are only called from the patrol thread). On read failures (corrupt JSON, missing file), return `null` and do not throw.

subItems.title: New file `src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation-state.ts` exports `StuckRemediationState` type, `readStuckRemediationState`, `writeStuckRemediationState`, `clearStuckRemediationState`
`readStuckRemediationState` returns `null` for a missing file (does not throw)
`readStuckRemediationState` returns `null` for a corrupt-JSON file (does not throw) and logs a warning via `console.warn`
`writeStuckRemediationState` creates the parent dir (`~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/`) if missing and writes pretty-printed JSON
`clearStuckRemediationState` is a no-op if the file does not exist
Unit tests in `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/stuck-remediation-state.test.ts` cover the four edge cases above using a tmp-dir override (no real `~/.panopticon` writes)

narrative.Action:
Create `src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation.ts` exporting `async function checkStuckAgentRemediation(opts?: { now?: number }): Promise<string[]>`. The function iterates running work agents (`listRunningAgentsSync()` filtered by `state.role === 'work'` and `state.status === 'running'`), and for each one applies the gate cascade described below. Returns the same `string[]` action list shape as other deacon patrols.

Gate cascade (all must pass before stage logic runs):
  1. Config: skip if `stuck_remediation.enabled === false`.
  2. Session: skip if `!sessionExistsSync(agentId)`.
  3. Status: skip if `state.paused || state.troubled || state.completedAt`.
  4. Tracker: skip if `reviewStatus.stuck`, `reviewStatus.deaconIgnored`, `reviewStatus.mergeStatus === 'merged'`, or `reviewStatus.readyForMerge`.
  5. Dead-end overlap: skip if `reviewStatus.reviewStatus` is `'blocked'` or `'failed'`, or `reviewStatus.verificationStatus === 'failed'`, or `reviewStatus.testStatus === 'failed'` (let `checkDeadEndAgents` handle those).
  6. Idle: skip if `!isAgentIdleForNudge(agentId)` (active/waiting-on-human/uninitialized-fresh agents are not idle).
  7. Bead overlap: skip if `bd ready -l <issueLabel>` returns any rows in the agent's workspace (let `nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads` handle those).

State handling:
  - Read `runtime.lastActivity` from `getAgentRuntimeStateSync(agentId)`. If missing, skip.
  - Read stored `stuckState = readStuckRemediationState(agentId)`.
  - If `stuckState` exists AND `runtime.lastActivity > stuckState.firstStuckAt` → `clearStuckRemediationState(agentId)` and continue (treat as fresh stall).
  - Compute `idleMinutes = (now - runtime.lastActivity) / 60_000`.

Stage transitions (highest applicable stage wins):
  - `idleMinutes >= stage3_minutes` AND `(stuckState?.lastStage ?? 0) < 3` → call `markAgentTroubled(agentId)`. Write `{ lastStage: 3, lastStageAt: now, firstStuckAt: stuckState?.firstStuckAt ?? runtime.lastActivity }`. Log `[deacon] stuck-remediation stage=3 issue=<id> idleMin=<n> action=marked-troubled`. Push to actions.
  - Else if `idleMinutes >= stage2_minutes` AND `(stuckState?.lastStage ?? 0) < 2` → call `resumeAgent(agentId, recoveryMessage)`. If `result.success` → write `{ lastStage: 2, lastStageAt: now, firstStuckAt }` and log/push action. If `!result.success` → DO NOT advance stored stage; log failure and let the next patrol retry.
  - Else if `idleMinutes >= stage1_minutes` AND `(stuckState?.lastStage ?? 0) < 1` → call `messageAgent(agentId, pokeMessage)`. Write `{ lastStage: 1, lastStageAt: now, firstStuckAt: stuckState?.firstStuckAt ?? new Date(runtime.lastActivity).toISOString() }`. Log/push action.
  - Otherwise no-op (waiting for the next threshold).

Messages (deterministic strings, no per-call variability):
  - Stage 1 poke: `"You appear stuck — no tool calls for ${idleMinutes} min. If your implementation is complete, run \`pan done ${issueId}\`. Otherwise reply with a one-line summary of what you're waiting on, then continue."`
  - Stage 2 resume: `"Resuming after auto-detected stall (${idleMinutes} min idle). Review your last work and decide whether to continue or signal done with \`pan done ${issueId}\`."`
  - Stage 3 has no message — it's silent gate-marking.

All exceptions wrapped per-agent in try/catch — one failing agent must not stop the loop for the others. Use `console.error` + `logDeaconEventSync` for unexpected throws.

subItems.title: New file `src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation.ts` exports `checkStuckAgentRemediation(opts?: { now?: number }): Promise<string[]>`
Function loads config via `loadCloisterConfigSync()` and returns `[]` immediately when `stuck_remediation.enabled === false`
All seven gates (config, session, status, tracker, dead-end overlap, idle, bead overlap) implemented and skip the agent without firing a stage
Activity-reset logic clears stored state and skips the cycle when `runtime.lastActivity > stuckState.firstStuckAt`
Stage 1 calls `messageAgent` with the exact poke message and persists `lastStage=1`
Stage 2 calls `resumeAgent` with the exact recovery message; persists `lastStage=2` ONLY if the resume succeeds
Stage 3 calls `markAgentTroubled`, persists `lastStage=3`, and never calls `messageAgent`/`resumeAgent` again on subsequent cycles for the same stall
Each stage transition emits a `[deacon] stuck-remediation stage=N issue=<id> idleMin=<n> action=<verb>` log via `logDeaconEventSync` and is included in the returned actions array
Per-agent try/catch: an exception in one agent's stage logic does not stop the loop for other agents
Function accepts optional `now?: number` argument used in place of `Date.now()` everywhere — for deterministic tests

items.title: Wire checkStuckAgentRemediation into runPatrol() in deacon.ts

narrative.Action: Import `checkStuckAgentRemediation` from `./stuck-remediation.js` in `src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts`. In `runPatrol()`, call it after `checkApiErrorAgents()` (currently line ~4689) and push its returned actions into `actions` + `addLog('action', a, state.patrolCycle)` exactly like the surrounding patrols. The patrol runs every cycle (no `Math.random()` gating).

subItems.title: `runPatrol()` calls `checkStuckAgentRemediation()` once per cycle after `checkApiErrorAgents()`
Returned actions are pushed into the `actions` array and each is logged via `addLog('action', a, state.patrolCycle)`
Global pause path (`isDeaconGloballyPaused()`) still short-circuits before the new call (no change to existing pause behaviour)
Existing deacon tests in `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/` still pass (no regression in `deacon-stuck-skip.test.ts`, `deacon-auto-closeout.test.ts`, etc.)

items.title: Unit tests for stage 1/2/3 transitions and gate cascade

narrative.Action: Add `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/stuck-remediation.test.ts` with a vitest suite that mocks `agents.js`, `tmux.js`, `review-status.js`, `config.js`, and `stuck-remediation-state.js`. Inject `opts.now` to simulate time advancing through the 20/45/90-min thresholds. Use `vi.useFakeTimers()` for any test that lets real wall-clock pass (per CLAUDE.md rule). Each test asserts on (a) which agent helper was called, (b) what was persisted to state, (c) what was logged.

subItems.title: Test: agent idle 5 min → no stage fires (below stage1 threshold)
Test: agent idle 25 min, no prior state → stage 1 fires; `messageAgent` called once with poke text; state persists `lastStage=1`
Test: agent idle 50 min, prior `lastStage=1` with no activity since → stage 2 fires; `resumeAgent` called with recovery text; state persists `lastStage=2`
Test: agent idle 50 min, prior `lastStage=1`, `resumeAgent` returns `{ success: false }` → state still `lastStage=1` (no advance on failure)
Test: agent idle 100 min, prior `lastStage=2` → stage 3 fires; `markAgentTroubled` called; state persists `lastStage=3`; `messageAgent` and `resumeAgent` NOT called
Test: agent idle 30 min, prior `lastStage=3` → no further action (no messageAgent, no resumeAgent, no markTroubled re-call)
Test: activity reset — agent had `lastStage=2` but `runtime.lastActivity > firstStuckAt` → `clearStuckRemediationState` called; no stage fires this cycle
Test: skip when `reviewStatus.stuck = true` (no stage fires regardless of idle minutes)
Test: skip when `reviewStatus.deaconIgnored = true`
Test: skip when `reviewStatus.reviewStatus === 'blocked'` (dead-end-agents owns it)
Test: skip when `bd ready -l <issue>` returns open beads (bead-nudge owns it)
Test: skip when `state.paused = true` or `state.troubled = true`
Test: skip when config `stuck_remediation.enabled = false`
Test: skip when `isAgentIdleForNudge` returns false (agent active or waiting-on-human)
Test: one agent throwing inside its stage logic does not stop processing of other agents in the same cycle
All tests use `vi.useFakeTimers()` or `opts.now` injection — zero real-wall-clock waits
